cuboid_volume_missing_dimension_value: Use the visible cuboid volume and dimensions.

cuboid_surface_area_value: Use the total surface-area formula for the cuboid.

cube_edge_from_frame_length_value: Use the bright highlighted cube-frame edges and their total length.
Use only the bright highlighted edges of the cube frame; the pale edges show the rest of the cube.
Use the highlighted frame-length readout and the marked cube edge.
Use the shown highlighted cube wire-frame measurement.
Use the bright highlighted cube-frame path.
open_box_net_dimension_value: Use the sheet dimensions and the equal corner cutouts.
Use the corner-cut net that folds into an open-top box.
Use the shown sheet, cutout, and base-panel information.

scene:rectangular_solid_scene.object_description: a labeled rectangular solid, cube frame, or open-box net with integer measurements and one missing value marker

task:cuboid_volume_missing_dimension_val.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to the target dimension guide segment as [[x0,y0],[x1,y1]]
task:cuboid_volume_missing_dimension_val.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested missing dimension as an integer

task:cuboid_surface_area_value.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to the pixel bounding box [x0,y0,x1,y1] around the cuboid
task:cuboid_surface_area_value.answer_hint: set "answer" to the total surface area as an integer

task:cube_edge_from_frame_length_value.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to the pixel bounding box [x0,y0,x1,y1] around the highlighted cube-frame path
task:cube_edge_from_frame_length_value.answer_hint: set "answer" to the cube edge length as an integer

task:open_box_net_dimension_value.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to the marked base-dimension segment as [[x0,y0],[x1,y1]]

query:open_box_dimension_from_corner_cut.answer_hint: set "answer" to the requested resulting base dimension as an integer

output:answer_only.json_output_contract_answer_only: Return a JSON object with exactly one key: "answer".

output:answer_and_annotation.json_output_contract: Return a JSON object with exactly two keys: "annotation" and "answer".
